titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/206

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=471 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:49:07.563746+00 | translation moa=472 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:49:05.195772+00

div.p:
Uniuersis christi fidelibus hoc scriptum uisuris uel audituris Thomas de Alneto Miles eternam salutem in domino . Nouerit vniuersitas uestra
me dedisse . concessisse et hac presenti carta mea confirmasse deo et beate Marie de Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus et in
perpetuum seruituris in liberam et perpetuam elemosinam terras de Brunsceth . de hauthyncref et de Dergauel per suas rectas diui
sas cum omnibus pertinenciis suis et quicquid imfra easdem diuisas continetur tam in hominibus quam pasturis . domibus . pratis . moris .
mariscis . aquis . molendinis . nemoribus et omnibus aliis . ita ut ipsi omnia premissa cum suis pertinentiis ita libere in perpetuum
teneant et habeant prout ea tenui . et in omnibus premissis nichil michi retinui . Immo Ego et successores mei omnia premissa eisdem
monachis in perpetuum warantizabimus . In cuius rei testimonium presenti scripto sigillum meum apposui . Hiis testibus . Iohanne de
Makuswell’ . Thomas filio Ranulfi vicecomite de Dunfres . Ionanne de Mundeuil’ . Waltero Croc . et Thoma Mautalent  militibus .
Laurencio de braccheley . Thoma de Cantia . et multis aliis . Datum Anno domini millesimo ducentesimo tricesimo septimo .

To all Christ's faithful who shall see or hear this written deed, Thomas de Aunay, knight, wishes eternal salvation in the Lord. Let your whole community know me to have given, granted and by this my present charter to have made firm to God and to Blessed Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place now and to come, for ever, in free and everlasting almsthe lands of ‘Brunsceth’, Auchencrieff, and Dargavel, by their correct boundaries, with all their pertinents and whatever is comprised within the same bounds as well in men as in pastures, buildings, meadows, moors, marshes, waters, mills, woods and all other things, so that they hold and have all the foregoing things with their pertinents so freely in perpetuity as I held them, and in all the foregoing things I have withheld nothing to myself. Indeed, I and my successors shall warrant all the foregoing to the same monks for ever. In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to the present written deed. With these witnesses: John of Maxwell; Thomas son of Ranulf, sheriff of Dumfries; John of Mandeville, Walter Crook, and Thomas Maitland, knights; Laurence of Brackley; Thomas of Kent; and many others. Given in the year of the Lord 1237.
